page: Amazon Web Services m9g.24xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 96 dedicated physical cores built on the ARMv9 architecture, utilizing the Neoverse-V3 model running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU hardware accelerators.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and data compression. It achieves top-tier status in stress-ng multi-core testing, static web serving throughput, and Redis SET operations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only cached blocks reaching top-tier performance and large uncached writes ranking in the top 25 percent. Additionally, the processor demonstrates strong capabilities in LLM inference for medium-sized models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARMv9 architecture and dedicated core allocation deliver high resource density and strong performance-per-resource metrics without the overhead of hyperthreading. The baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps supports high-throughput data transfers. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m9g.24xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server instance powered by 96 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 384.0 GB of memory, translating to 4.0 GB per core, and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the m9g.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high-throughput database operations, including Redis SET execution. Its ARMv9 architecture provides strong qualitative cost efficiency by delivering high resource density and performance-per-resource without hyperthreading. This instance is optimized for demanding multi-threaded workloads, high-volume web serving, database management, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.24xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 96 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V3 cores, 384 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for database and multi-threaded workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring ninety-six dedicated cores and high memory bandwidth for demanding multi-threaded and database workloads.

384.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
